title: Evangelion Chronicle artbooks Is there any difference
display_title: Evangelion Chronicle artbooks Is there any difference
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion Chronicle artbooks. Is there any difference             Quote Post by Collossus » Fri Sep 06, 2013 2:50 pm Hello, I was thinking about buying Evangelion Chornicle artbooks and I noticed than there are at least three: http://www.ebay.com/itm/Evangelion-Chronicle-Illustrations-/171119251829?pt=AU_Non_Fiction_Books_2&hash=item27d7809175 http://www.ebay.com/itm/JAPAN-Evangelion-Chronicle-Illustrations-New-Version-art-book-2011-/310682873693?pt=LH_DefaultDomain_0&hash=item4856246f5d www.ebay.com/itm/Evangelion-chronicle-The-Essential-Side-B-data-art-book-/310737121901?pt=LH_DefaultDomain_0&hash=item485960326d Is it worth to buy both of them? If not, which one is better? Thanks a lot.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Sep 06, 2013 3:02 pm Asuka cover includes all of the material from Rei cover (including posters), but adds NME material. The only unique part of the Rei version is the cover. The Side B collection is an entirely different book. The first two are just art, while the other is full of text information and such. I would imagine the question of whether or not it's better than the other turns on the question of whether or not you can read Japanese.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Fri Sep 06, 2013 3:52 pm There are some people who would buy both the Rei and Asuka versions from a collecting standpoint. This happened to me with Die Sterne as there was a 4 year gap between the initial release and Ver. 2.0. Sometimes it pays to wait on art book releases. Now for some reason Udon opted to publish the Rei version in English but not the updated Asuka one. They are supposed to publish both Side A and B later this year but no firm date yet so I would wait on that.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Sep 06, 2013 4:16 pm AR-99 wrote: There are some people who would buy both the Rei and Asuka versions from a collecting standpoint. This happened to me with Die Sterne as there was a 4 year gap between the initial release and Ver. 2.0. Sometimes it pays to wait on art book releases.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Fri Sep 06, 2013 5:14 pm I wish there was more of a market to justify additional art book localization into English.

[]:             Quote Post by Collossus » Fri Sep 06, 2013 5:43 pm www.bookdepository.co.uk/Evangelion-Chronicle-Illustrations-WEVE-Inc/9781926778556 This updated version of Rei's one has the same number of pages of Asuka's one. Do they have the same content, except for the cover?

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Fri Sep 06, 2013 5:54 pm Interesting. I'd shoot Udon an email and ask them what version they published just to be sure.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Sep 06, 2013 6:09 pm AR-99 wrote: Interesting. I'd shoot Udon an email and ask them what version they published just to be sure.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Fri Sep 06, 2013 6:14 pm Apology offered and thanks for the breakdown.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Sep 07, 2013 4:14 pm Bagheera wrote: So Japanese Rei version is obsolete, English Rei version is for those of us who don't grok the moon runes, and Japanese Asuka version is for extra (mostly NTE) content.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Sep 07, 2013 4:21 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Could you please explain what the Udon release means by this? "Plus new pieces produced exclusively for this volume."

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Dec 28, 2014 4:15 pm Originally I was thinking of getting Die Sterne 2.0 (since I loved my original copy so much--had to sell it for space/money reasons) but something about the Chronicle Illustrations is pretty appealing, too. Maybe it's because the pages seem like they'd be so glossy? I dunno. Only thing with nabbing the Asuka/revised version, is I wouldn't be surprised at all if they'll continue the Chronicle issues for 3.0 and FINAL sometime--and later release a Mari cover variant/new revision because of it. So, do I just nab the Asuka version when I can, or wait a few years on the off chance they'll be another revision down the line? I used to be sort of into collecting, but I could never afford to double-dip nor have much of a collecting mindset these days. I just want what little things I'll enjoy. I'm just cautious because years ago I bought myself (and later sold? I forget) Entry File 1 (1.0 proper, information/art book) under the impression I'd just collect all 4 books so I could own a little piece of New Theatrical Edition--but of course, Studio khara/whoever never released Entry File 2 onwards. No explanation given. :(

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Sun Dec 28, 2014 6:36 pm Personally I'm happy with the UDON release being a straight translation of the Rei version. To me, it seems kind of weird mixing NGE with NTE content a la the Asuka cover. The two should be kept separate for their own releases, and as SSD stated above, they're most likely just going to roll out another updated iteration to cover any possible Q or Shin content at a later juncture. Also, all my Eva merch is strictly NGE, kind of like an unwritten rule I've set myself. I can't be bothered with any of the new movie stuff unless it really catches my eye.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Dec 28, 2014 6:45 pm Hm, that is a good point (and I'm fine with NGE and NTE being kept separate in artbooks, media releases, etc). Maybe I should just get the English/original release then? Just not sure! I'm just really itchin' for an Eva artbook, and one that's not crazy overpriced/import fees. (Part of the problem with re-buying DS or getting the 2.0 updated artbook.) Der Mond didn't appeal to me much the brief time I owned it...

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Dec 28, 2014 9:06 pm Get the English Asuka release. You get more content, and if they come up with another release later on you're no worse off than you were before. I say this as someone who's gotten both original releases and not terribly regretted it.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Dec 28, 2014 9:26 pm You mean the Japanese Asuka release? There is no English version of it out (yet?), just that Rei release by Udon. I'll think 'em over with which one I'd want, thanks. :) Probably won't buy it for awhile either.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Dec 28, 2014 9:41 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: You mean the Japanese Asuka release? There is no English version of it out (yet?), just that Rei release by Udon.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Mon Dec 29, 2014 1:06 am Given that Udon's Eva footprint is tiny (only Chronicles Illustrations and Side A and B, of which A is finally getting released this March despite being announced for release last year), I don't expect them to release an Asuka version any time soon so you may be waiting a bit.
